The **Basketball Africa League** (**BAL**) is the premier men's [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") league in [Africa](/wiki/Africa "Africa").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Founded in 2019, the league was established and is co-organized by [NBA Africa](/wiki/NBA_Africa "NBA Africa") and the [International Basketball Federation](/wiki/FIBA "FIBA") (FIBA).[[2]](#cite_note-USATODAY_12_Teams-2)

Each season typically runs from March to June, and in the current format of the league consists of twelve teams. Each of the teams qualify through their performance in their domestic competition.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Seven teams qualify directly, while five others have to play in the [Road to BAL](/wiki/Road_to_BAL "Road to BAL"). The group stage is divided into three conferences, which are followed by playoffs to determine the champions. The BAL champions of each season automatically qualify for the [FIBA Intercontinental Cup](/wiki/FIBA_Intercontinental_Cup "FIBA Intercontinental Cup").

The first season was held in [2021](/wiki/2021_BAL_season "2021 BAL season") and thus far, five different champions have won the competition.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketball_Africa_League&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

On 16 February 2019 the [National Basketball Association](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association") and [FIBA](/wiki/FIBA "FIBA") announced plans to establish a [continental](/wiki/Africa "Africa") professional basketball league.[[4]](#cite_note-4) During a press conference at the [2019 NBA All-Star weekend](/wiki/2019_NBA_All-Star_Game "2019 NBA All-Star Game"), NBA commissioner [Adam Silver](/wiki/Adam_Silver "Adam Silver") elaborated on plans to establish the league. He stated that the league will feature 12 teams after qualification tournaments in late 2019. The countries that could possibly host a team include: [Angola](/wiki/Angola "Angola"), [Egypt](/wiki/Egypt "Egypt"), [Kenya](/wiki/Kenya "Kenya"), [Morocco](/wiki/Morocco "Morocco"), [Nigeria](/wiki/Nigeria "Nigeria"), [Rwanda](/wiki/Rwanda "Rwanda"), [Senegal](/wiki/Senegal "Senegal"), [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa") and [Tunisia](/wiki/Tunisia "Tunisia").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) Silver also hinted at the involvement of former [U.S. president](/wiki/Presidency_of_Barack_Obama "Presidency of Barack Obama") [Barack Obama](/wiki/Barack_Obama "Barack Obama") in an unspecified role.[[2]](#cite_note-USATODAY_12_Teams-2) In May 2019, [Amadou Gallo Fall](/wiki/Amadou_Gallo_Fall "Amadou Gallo Fall") was assigned by the NBA as the first president of the BAL.[[7]](#cite_note-7) In September 2019, BAL announced the venues and cities for the inaugural season, which included a Final Four played in [Kigali Arena](/wiki/Kigali_Arena "Kigali Arena") in [Kigali](/wiki/Kigali "Kigali"), Rwanda.[[8]](#cite_note-forbes-8)

On 15 October 2019, the [qualifying tournaments](/wiki/2020_BAL_Qualifying_Tournaments "2020 BAL Qualifying Tournaments") for the [inaugural season](/wiki/2020_BAL_season "2020 BAL season") began, with teams from 32 African countries participating.[[9]](#cite_note-9) The start of the BAL was eventually postponed twice due to the disruptive [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_Africa "COVID-19 pandemic in Africa").[[10]](#cite_note-10) The first BAL game was played on 16 May 2021, in the [Kigali Arena](/wiki/Kigali_Arena "Kigali Arena") in Kigali, Rwanda, which hosted the entire tournament. The season was held in a [bio-secure bubble](/wiki/Bio-secure_bubble "Bio-secure bubble") with only limited attendance allowed. On 30 May, [Zamalek](/wiki/Zamalek_SC_(basketball) "Zamalek SC (basketball)") from Egypt won the first-ever BAL championship after beating [US Monastir](/wiki/US_Monastir_(basketball) "US Monastir (basketball)") in the [inaugural final](/wiki/2021_BAL_Finals "2021 BAL Finals").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

The following season, the league expanded it format with five-team conferences (the Sahara and Nile Conference), which were held in [Dakar](/wiki/Dakar "Dakar") and [Cairo](/wiki/Cairo "Cairo"). The playoffs were held in Kigali.

The fourth league season, in [2024](/wiki/2024_BAL_season "2024 BAL season"), saw an expansion to three conference with the [Kalahari Conference](/wiki/Kalahari_Conference "Kalahari Conference"),[[12]](#cite_note-:4-12) as well as [Petro de Luanda](/wiki/Petro_de_Luanda_(basketball) "Petro de Luanda (basketball)") becoming the first team from [Sub-Saharan Africa](/wiki/Sub-Saharan_Africa "Sub-Saharan Africa") to win the championship.[[13]](#cite_note-:3-13) After the season, [Ulrich Chomche](/wiki/Ulrich_Chomche "Ulrich Chomche") from Cameroon became the first former BAL player to be drafted in the [National Basketball Association](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association") (NBA), as he was picked in the second round of the [2024 NBA draft](/wiki/2024_NBA_draft "2024 NBA draft").[[14]](#cite_note-14)

The [2025](/wiki/2025_BAL_season "2025 BAL season") season was won by [Al Ahli Tripoli](/wiki/Al_Ahli_Tripoli_(basketball) "Al Ahli Tripoli (basketball)"), who were the first team to advance all the way through the qualifying rounds to win the championship. The league also hosted its first finals outside of Rwanda, with the South African city of [Pretoria](/wiki/Pretoria "Pretoria") being the host.[[15]](#cite_note-15) On June 25, 2025, [Khaman Maluach](/wiki/Khaman_Maluach "Khaman Maluach") was drafted 10th overall in the [2025 NBA draft](/wiki/2025_NBA_draft "2025 NBA draft"), becoming the highest former BAL player to be drafted into the [NBA](/wiki/NBA "NBA").[[16]](#cite_note-16)

In September 2025, NBA Commissioner [Adam Silver](/wiki/Adam_Silver "Adam Silver") announced the intention of the league to move to a closed league with permanent franchises.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

## Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketball_Africa_League&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Format")]

Each BAL season consists of twelve teams. In the regular season, the twelve teams are divided into three conferences (the [Sahara Conference](/wiki/Sahara_Conference "Sahara Conference"), [Nile Conference](/wiki/Nile_Conference "Nile Conference"), and [Kalahari Conference](/wiki/Kalahari_Conference "Kalahari Conference")),[[12]](#cite_note-:4-12) in which they each team plays the other in a round-robin format.

The two highest-placed teams of each conference, as well as the two best third-ranked teams, advance to the playoffs, which is a single-elimination tournament which decides the BAL champion.

### Qualification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketball_Africa_League&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Qualification")]

Main article: [Road to BAL](/wiki/Road_to_BAL "Road to BAL")

Similar to the [Basketball Champions League](/wiki/Basketball_Champions_League "Basketball Champions League") and [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") competition [CAF Champions League](/wiki/CAF_Champions_League "CAF Champions League"), the BAL has qualifying rounds to determine the teams that qualify for each season. [National federations](/wiki/FIBA_Africa "FIBA Africa") from African countries are given the opportunity to send one representing club, usually the champions of the respective national league. In the [Road to BAL](/wiki/Road_to_BAL "Road to BAL"), teams play each other in groups for six remaining spots in the BAL regular season. In the first round, all participating teams are divided over groups, with the top teams advancing to the Elite 16. There, another group stage follows before single-game eliminations are played in the semi-finals and finals.

Six teams from six predetermined countries qualify directly for the regular season, without playing qualifiers, to make for a total of twelve teams. These countries have been determined based on basketball history and commercial market size, and have thus far been Angola, Egypt, Tunisia, Morocco (only in 2021), Nigeria, Rwanda (since 2022) and Senegal.

### Foreign players restrictions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketball_Africa_League&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Foreign players restrictions")]

From 2021 to 2024, each club participating in the BAL regular season was restricted to having four foreign players on its team, which means it has to have at least eight local players on its roster. Furthermore, two out of four foreign players had to be from another African country. A maximum of 2 out of the 4 foreign players can be from outside of Africa.[[18]](#cite_note-18) Since the 2024 season, clubs have been able to field more foreign players.

### BAL Elevate program

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketball_Africa_League&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: BAL Elevate program")]

Ahead of the [2022 season](/wiki/2022_BAL_season "2022 BAL season"), the BAL launched the BAL Elevate program in cooperation with the [NBA Academy Africa](/wiki/NBA_Academy_Africa "NBA Academy Africa"). The program places a player from the academy in one of the team's rosters. Players from the same country as a BAL team are automatically assigned to a team, while remaining players are selected from a pool. Elevate players do not share in the prize money that is awarded from the competition, to preserve their amateur status.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

## Records and statistics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketball_Africa_League&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Records and statistics")]

Main article: [List of BAL records](/wiki/List_of_BAL_records "List of BAL records")

[Solo Diabate](/wiki/Solo_Diabate "Solo Diabate") and [Michael Fakuade](/wiki/Michael_Fakuade "Michael Fakuade") have won two BAL championships, and are the only players to have won multiple titles.[[20]](#cite_note-20) [Chris Crawford](/wiki/Chris_Crawford_(basketball,_born_1992) "Chris Crawford (basketball, born 1992)") is the all-time scoring leader of the league, having scored 341 points in three seasons.[*[outdated statistic](/wiki/Wikipedia:Avoid_weasel_words "Wikipedia:Avoid weasel words")*]

## Sponsorship and partnerships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketball_Africa_League&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Sponsorship and partnerships")]

Since its inception, the BAL has been sponsored by multiple multinational organisations, including:
[Air Jordan](/wiki/Air_Jordan "Air Jordan"), [Flutterwave](/wiki/Flutterwave "Flutterwave"), [French Development Agency](/wiki/French_Development_Agency "French Development Agency") (AFD), [Hennessy](/wiki/Hennessy "Hennessy")[[21]](#cite_note-23), [New Fortress Energy](/wiki/New_Fortress_Energy "New Fortress Energy")[[22]](#cite_note-24), [Nike](/wiki/Nike,_Inc. "Nike, Inc."), [RwandAir](/wiki/RwandAir "RwandAir")[[23]](#cite_note-25), [Visit Rwanda](/wiki/Rwanda_Development_Board "Rwanda Development Board")[[24]](#cite_note-26), and [Wilson](/wiki/Wilson_Sporting_Goods "Wilson Sporting Goods")

## Trophy and prize money

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketball_Africa_League&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Trophy and prize money")]

The champions of the BAL finals receive the competitions' trophy, which is inspired by the [adansonia](/wiki/Adansonia "Adansonia") (more commonly known as baobab), a common type of tree in Africa.[[25]](#cite_note-27) Since the 2025 season, the players and coaches on the championship team also receive [championship rings](/wiki/Championship_ring "Championship ring"), following a North American sports tradition.[[26]](#cite_note-28)

In the inaugural season in 2021, the champions reportedly received $100,000 in [prize money](/wiki/Prize_money "Prize money"); runners-up received $75,000; third place $55,000 and fourth place $25,000.[[27]](#cite_note-29)

## Organisation, ownership and investments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketball_Africa_League&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Organisation, ownership and investments")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e3/Amadou_Gallo_Fall.png/250px-Amadou_Gallo_Fall.png)](/wiki/File:Amadou_Gallo_Fall.png)

Amadou Gallo Fall is the first President of the BAL, since assuming the position in 2021

The BAL is owned and operated by NBA Africa, a sub-entity of the [NBA](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association") which was established in May 2021.[[28]](#cite_note-30) Since then, the league has been driven by private investments. At the moment of establishment, strategic investors including a consortium of Babatunde “Tunde” Folawiyo, Helios Fairfax Partners Corporation (HFP). Other investors included former players such as [Dikembe Mutombo](/wiki/Dikembe_Mutombo "Dikembe Mutombo"), [Junior Bridgeman](/wiki/Junior_Bridgeman "Junior Bridgeman"), [Luol Deng](/wiki/Luol_Deng "Luol Deng"), [Grant Hill](/wiki/Grant_Hill "Grant Hill"), [Joakim Noah](/wiki/Joakim_Noah "Joakim Noah") and [Ian Mahinmi](/wiki/Ian_Mahinmi "Ian Mahinmi").[[29]](#cite_note-31) Two months later, it was announced that former President of the United States [Barack Obama](/wiki/Barack_Obama "Barack Obama") joined NBA Africa as a strategic partner while also purchasing a minority stake in the organisation.[[30]](#cite_note-32) NBA commissioner [Adam Silver](/wiki/Adam_Silver "Adam Silver") stated that NBA Africa was valued at $1 billion.[[31]](#cite_note-33)

The organisation of the league currently exists out of:

* Amadou Gallo Fall (Commissioner)[[32]](#cite_note-34)
* John Manyo-Plange (Vice-President)[[33]](#cite_note-35)
* Victor Williams (CEO NBA Africa)

### Criticism and controversies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketball_Africa_League&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Criticism and controversies")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/66/Le_pr%C3%A9sident_du_Rwanda_Paul_Kagame_lors_de_la_c%C3%A9r%C3%A9monie_de_d%C3%A9voilement_du_logo_BAL_%C3%A0_la_Kigali_Arena%2C_%C3%A0_Kigali%2C_Rwanda%2C_le_19_d%C3%A9cembre_2019.jpg/250px-Le_pr%C3%A9sident_du_Rwanda_Paul_Kagame_lors_de_la_c%C3%A9r%C3%A9monie_de_d%C3%A9voilement_du_logo_BAL_%C3%A0_la_Kigali_Arena%2C_%C3%A0_Kigali%2C_Rwanda%2C_le_19_d%C3%A9cembre_2019.jpg)](/wiki/File:Le_pr%C3%A9sident_du_Rwanda_Paul_Kagame_lors_de_la_c%C3%A9r%C3%A9monie_de_d%C3%A9voilement_du_logo_BAL_%C3%A0_la_Kigali_Arena,_%C3%A0_Kigali,_Rwanda,_le_19_d%C3%A9cembre_2019.jpg)

President of Rwanda [Paul Kagame](/wiki/Paul_Kagame "Paul Kagame") at a ceremony for the unveiling of the BAL logo in Kigali in 2018

The [Human Rights Foundation](/wiki/Human_Rights_Foundation "Human Rights Foundation") has criticized the BAL for maintaining close relations with [Paul Kagame](/wiki/Paul_Kagame "Paul Kagame") and his Rwandan authoritarian government, and has sent an official letter to the [NBA](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association") regarding the matter.[[34]](#cite_note-:0-36) Other newspapers such as *[The Guardian](/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")* and *[Bloomberg News](/wiki/Bloomberg_News "Bloomberg News")* have also described the league as being a tool for [sportswashing](/wiki/Sportswashing "Sportswashing") for the Rwandan government's [repression](/wiki/Human_rights_in_Rwanda "Human rights in Rwanda") and the regime's involvement in the [Rwandan Civil War](/wiki/Rwandan_Civil_War "Rwandan Civil War").[[34]](#cite_note-:0-36)[[35]](#cite_note-37)[[36]](#cite_note-38)
